mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP and corporate revenue.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment typically precedes or accompanies economic downturns.
failure_condition: If, during periods where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is declining while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable or also declines, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy without inflationary pressures, which supports equity valuations by lowering discount rates and improving economic outlook, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change < -0.25% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change > -0.2% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy easing typically supports asset prices.
failure_condition: If the combination of declining FEDFUNDS and stable CPIAUCSL does not yield statistically significant positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.1) in at least 70% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) during a period of rising interest rates signals a significant slowdown in the housing market and broader economic demand, which negatively impacts construction-related sectors and consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a key economic indicator.
failure_condition: If, during periods of rising interest rates and declining HOUST, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating above its long-term trend indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling strong economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of economic output and manufacturing health.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 5% annualized over 3 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a primary driver of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is decelerating significantly while retail sales (RSAFS) are also contracting, it indicates a broad-based weakening of aggregate demand, which severely impacts corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth < 1% AND RSAFS 3-month change < -1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and retail sales are direct measures of economic demand.
failure_condition: If, during periods of combined GDPC1 deceleration and RSAFS contraction, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising Federal Funds Rate during a period of already elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation, which increases the cost of capital and reduces economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
evidence_refs: fact:Aggressive rate hikes typically precede market corrections.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS is above 5% and CPIAUCSL is above 4%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, the hypothesis is rejected.
